blocks.text: made a note. Wren saw things. She had been doing it all weekend.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I'm going to head up. Long day.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Yeah. Stay. Don't let me kill the night.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: We'll be up in a bit.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: No rush. I'll sleep better knowing you're all down here being loud.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: We should probably head back too.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: One more round of sparkling cider first.
9:17pm. The voices started through the ceiling.
Two of them. One was Marcus. The other — harder to place from below.
Not shouting. Worse than shouting. The controlled kind that people use when they don't want the floor to hear.

blocks.prompt: The room has gone quiet. What does Julian do?

options.label: He sits very still and listens.
He watches the group's faces instead.
